text:
In an action against an owner or operator of a valet parking service that has not established financial responsibility as required by this chapter to recover damages for personal injuries, death, or property damage sustained in a motor vehicle collision arising out of the operation of a valet parking service, it is not a defense that the party who brings the action:

(1) was guilty of contributory negligence; or

(2) assumed the risk of injury, death, or property damage.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 686. Valet Parking Svcs.
##### § 686.005 — Common Law Defenses

In an action against an owner or operator of a valet parking service that has not established financial responsibility as required by this chapter to recover damages for personal injuries, death, or property damage sustained in a motor vehicle collision arising out of the operation of a valet parking service, it is not a defense that the party who brings the action:

    (1) was guilty of contributory negligence; or

    (2) assumed the risk of injury, death, or property damage.

*Acts 2023, 88th Leg., R.S., Ch. 709 (H.B.2190), Sec. 100, eff. September 1, 2023.*

subsections.text: In an action against an owner or operator of a valet parking service that has not established financial responsibility as required by this chapter to recover damages for personal injuries, death, or property damage sustained in a motor vehicle collision arising out of the operation of a valet parking service, it is not a defense that the party who brings the action:
was guilty of contributory negligence; or
assumed the risk of injury, death, or property damage.
